title: Pricing
description: A snapshot of pricing observations for a single asset from a single producer. The platform aggregates data items by (asset, dataType=pricing, source); each subsequent record from the same source overwrites the producer's previous snapshot. Multi-producer aggregation falls out naturally — different producers contribute distinct (asset, dataType=pricing, source) rows. Producers carrying multiple pricing aspects (e.g. bid + ask + last, or NAV + oracle price) ship them all in one snapshot via the `values` map keyed by semantic price name. Per-value timestamp and signature are preserved.

timestamp.description: Producer timestamp at which the snapshot was emitted, epoch milliseconds. Used as the default for any value entry that omits its own `timestamp`.

values.description: Map of valueType → observation. Keys are semantic price names so consumers can look up a known type across producers. Expected vocabulary: "nav", "fairValue", "oraclePrice", "peg", "indicative" (declared values); "last", "bid", "ask", "mid", "vwap" (market quotes); "open", "high", "low", "close", "previousClose" (session marks); "settlement", "markPrice" (derivatives); "cleanPrice", "dirtyPrice", "ytm", "ytw", "duration" (bond analytics). New keys may be added as use cases arise; consumers should ignore unrecognised keys.

value.description: Raw value, scaled by `decimal`. Strings are permitted to preserve precision for uint64-sized values that overflow IEEE 754 (~2^53).

decimal.description: Scale factor — human-readable value = value / 10^decimal. 0 for integer values.

timestamp.description: Optional. Per-value timestamp in epoch milliseconds. Falls back to the snapshot's top-level `timestamp` if absent.

signature.description: Optional. DER-encoded signature over the observation, when the producer is a signing oracle. Passed through verbatim for downstream verification.
